rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VALENTINA E. SNYDER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Reconsideration Of The Order Dated February 26, 2020 With Supplemental/Additional Objections To Amendment And Supplement To Petition For Approval Of Third Interim Account And Report Of Executor Responses To Minutes Dated February 19, 2020 And To Probate Examiner'S Notes Dated November 20, 2019, Decmber 24, 2019 And January 2, 2020 And Proposed Orders
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/30/20. No earlier date available. For the court to reconsider a ruling, a motion must be filed; however, a motion for reconsideration is ordinarily filed by the party whose petition was adjudicated, not by objectors to the petition. Here, the petitioners of this "petitio for reconsideration" appear to be seeking an appeal of the order signed on 2/25/20 and filed on 2/26/20. An appeal cannot be filed in original the trial court. The proof of service (Civil) is the incorrect form; Probate matters requre a Notice of Hearing, on the reverse side of which is the correct proof of service. (Furthermore, the incorrenct proof of serivce is also incomplete.) An original, unfiled "Petition for Reconsideration," etc., signed by Yolanda Dela Saflor was delivered to the Court. Examiners will not review unfiled papers. Ms. Saflor (or someone on her behalf) must pick up the papers from Room 202. If that petition is not picked up by 8/31/20, it will be recycled.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF OPHELIA C. HILTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Payment Of Fees And Costs To Conservator Of The Person And To Her Attorney
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/28/2020 (no earlier date available). 1) The following issues remain unaddressed since the previous tentative ruling: A) The proof of service shows an old address of Caroline Hinshaw whose request for special notice is on file. The address change notice was filed on 07/09/2019. B) The proof of service shows a different address for Marc Seidenfeld whose request for special notice is on file, but no address change notice has been filed by the requester. C) When did the petitioner increase her hourly rate to $145? Did the petitioner file a proposed new hourly schedule or statement of compensation? D) According to the prior fee petition (filed 03/10/2011), the petitioner requested compensation for her services through 03/06/2011 and obtained court approval. Why is the petitioner's time and service log including her services from 08/30/2010 through 02/22/2011? E) Some dates of the petitioner's visits to the conservatee do not appear on the status reports. 2) The following issue must be addressed: Has the conservatee's probate been opened? If so, have the petitioner and her attorney filed creditor claims for their fees?

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF THEA ARCHILLETTI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/21/2020 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) What are the total hours spent by the petitioner's attorney and his paralegal after 03/07/2018 on preparation of the petitioner/attorney's fee declarations and the attorney's invoices? The attorney's invoices show multiple tasks billed in a single entry (blocked billings) on some dates or only refer to "amended third account" without specifying whether the work was related to the addition of the petitioner/attorney's fees or other amendments. 2) According to the related trust account #3, the petitioner settled the conservatee's personal injury claim against Agape Transport in December 2017 or January 2018. Did the petitioner obtain court approval before settling the claim? See Probate Code section 2504(c). Also, why were the settlement proceeds paid to the trust instead of the conservatorship? 3) Was the conservatee's personal injury claim appraised as an additional asset and inventoried as such before the petitioner settled the claim? 4) The requirements of section 2628(a)(2)&(3) must be addressed in addition to the estate value.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANDREW GEORGE JAMESON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/21/2020 (no earlier date available). A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: 1) The allegation regarding creditors' claims must include the information required by CRC 7.403(3)&(5). 2) The caption states that this is the 7th report, but the court's register of actions shows that this is the 8th report. Notice of hearing must also state the "complete title" of the pleading ("8th" report of status of administration). 3) What is the date of sale and price of each sold item listed on the inventory and appraisal? 4) What is the amount of cash on hand? 5) Has the petitioner taken any IAEA actions without prior court approval? If so, all information required by CRC 7.250(a) must be reported.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE THEA T. ARCHILLETTI REVOCABLE TRUST ESTABLISHED PURSUANT TO COURT ORDER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/21/2020 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) Paragraph 12 of the petition: Is "UCSF Foundation" the correct name of a beneficiary? The governing trust instrument refers to "UCSF Helen Diller Family Comprehensive Cancer Center". 2) What is "Alamo Ins Settlement" described as a disbursement item on 02/02/2018? 3) Each category of disbursements must be sub-totaled. CRC 7.575(c). 4) The amendment to the trust signed by the petitioner has not been filed. 5) The petitioner's timesheets show 5 hours to "Complete Third Trust Account" on 01/16/2019 (did the petitioner mean 2020?). Specific descriptions of services the petitioner performed to complete the account must be provided, together with a breakdown of hours spent on each category of service. 6) Why did the petitioner's attorney and his paralegal have to spend 7 hours to amend this petition? 7) What are the total hours spent by the petitioner's attorney and his paralegal after 01/10/2020 on preparation of the petitioner/attorney's fee declarations and the attorney's invoices? The attorney's invoices show multiple tasks billed in a single entry (blocked billings) on some dates or only refer to "amended third account" without specifying whether the work was related to the addition of the petitioner/attorney's fees or other amendments.

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF KRISTINA MOLLOY MILLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/21/2020 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) The account statement (filed 08/03/2020) covers the period from 10/01/2019 through 12/31/2019, but the ending date of this account is 09/30/2019. An original account statement covering the preceding period must be filed. 2) Original account statements verifying the balance immediately preceding the date of the petitioner's appointment as temporary guardian (08/06/2018) have not been filed. Probate Code section 2620(c)(2). 3) A copy of the receipt for the $75 cost reimbursement sought by the petitioner was not included in Exhibit "A" to the supplement. 4) The beginning carry value must be the inventory and appraisal value as of the date of the petitioner's appointment as temporary guardian (08/07/2018). The inventory and appraisal (filed 02/26/2019) shows the general appointment date. A corrected inventory and appraisal showing the assets in existence and their values as of 08/07/2018 must be filed. 5) Has the petitioner forwarded the ongoing Minnesota litigation information to the referee for appraisal? See the Probate Referee Guide ("Judgments and Ongoing Litigation"). Paragraph 2 of the supplement alleges that disputed accounts are listed as a current asset in Schedule A in the accounting, but Schedule A only lists "Pension Benefit" and "UTMA Account Balance Transfer". 6) The amount of the minor's pension income reported on her 2018 return far exceeds the amount of the proceeds from the pension benefit reported on Schedule A. Paragraph 7 of the supplement alleges that the minor received "monthly" pension checks, but no monthly receipts or lump sum proceeds representing the uncashed monthly checks are reported in this account. 7) A copy of a receipt showing the $60 filing fee paid by the petitioner's attorney on 03/21/2018 must be filed. The Court's record does not show any payment on that date. 8) Paragraph 12 of the supplement needs to include the information required by CRC 7.703(e)(4)&(5).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PEARL CALHOUN
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (474 La Grande Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94112)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required pursuant to CRC 7.452. See also LRSF 14.3(A)(3) requiring counsel's appearance even if a continuance is needed to correct procedural issues. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proof of mailing of notice of the hearing is on file. 2) No proof of the clerk's posting of notice of the hearing is on file. 3) The published notice does not state that the petitioner granted an exclusive listing to Keller Williams. LRSF 14.55B. 4) The published notice misspelled the street name of the property ("Lagrnde" instead of "La Grande"). 5) Item 4a of the petition states that the first name of one of the buyers is "Jim", but according to the purchase agreement attached to the petition, it's "Jin". 6) Item 8c of the petition was not checked. 7) Item 10 miscalculates the minimum overbid. 8)Item 13 is blank. 9) No proposed orders have been submitted. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NORMAN M. STEWART SR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. remote appearance highly encouraged. Please review the 6th amended general order for information on appearing by CourtCall and see below for Zoom information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. ISSUES (to be addressed at hearing): 1) Petitioner checked Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary. Petitioner also checked Letters of Administration and requested Petitioner be appointed administrator. 2) Petitioner alleges the Lost Will was a pour over will to the Stewart Living Trust which is also lost. Petitioner says the presumption of California Probate Code 6124 do not apply as Decedent did not intended to revoke or destroy the Stewart Living Trust. Unclear if there are probate assets. The Declaration of real property shows the only property as 1734 Lyon Street the same property that was Quitclaimed into the Trust on 02/05/2004.
